description: ADR-039 illustrative -- Movate fleet COST pane: $/customer ranked, tokens by provider, $/run trend, cost outliers. Tagged 'illustrative -- pending ADR 039 sign-off; assumes a customer template variable resolves either via Lighthouse subscription scope (Phase 1) or via the customer OTel attribute (Phase 2)'. Source: mdk.run.tokens / mdk.run.cost_usd (metric-side; tenant only) + agent.execute span (provider, model_override) for the per-provider split (mirrors PR #518's mdk-cost.json reasoning).

options.content:
### Cost -- runbook

**What this pane answers:** $/customer (ANONYMIZED in the public/share view -- the hashed `customer` value from ADR 039 D4 is what the ranked bar shows), tokens by provider, $/run trend, and cost OUTLIERS by z-score.

**Normal:** $/customer ranking is stable -- top spenders correlate with usage. $/run trend is flat or trending down (model substitution, prompt-shortening wins). No customer sits >2sigma above its own 7-day median.

**If red:**
* **One customer 3sigma above their own median** -- check `usage.json` for a volume spike, and `health.json` for a retry storm (high non-success rate -> wasted token spend). Cost outliers driven by retries are an ADR 034 dead-letter discipline failure, not a usage signal.
* **A provider line jumps in 'tokens by provider'** -- check the routing config; a fallback to a more expensive model (e.g. a small-model timeout cascading to a large-model retry) shows here first.
* **Fleet $/run trends up** -- product-level: model adoption shifted, or new agents are more verbose. Surface in product review.

**Hidden assumptions** (from `docs/observability.md`): `mdk.run.tokens` and `mdk.run.cost_usd` carry ONLY a `tenant` attribute (no `provider`, no `agent`). The provider split MUST come from the `agent.execute` span (`provider` attribute) -- it's not on the metric. Mirror of PR #518's `mdk-cost.json`.

**Cross-refs:** ADR 015 (Langfuse for per-run drilldown), ADR 036 (usage metering + quotas), ADR 034 (DLQ -- retry-cost discipline).

panels.description: Ranked $/customer over the window. The `customer` label is the hashed identifier from ADR 039 D4; the bar chart is anonymized by design -- joining back to a real customer requires the salt + lookup table, kept out of Grafana.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
AppMetrics
| where Name == 'mdk.run.cost_usd'
| extend cust = tostring(Properties['customer'])
| where cust in ($customer) or '$customer' == 'All'
| summarize Spend = sum(ValueSum) by cust
| top 25 by Spend desc

panels.title: Top 25 customers by spend (anonymized)

panels.description: Tokens by provider over time. Built from a JOIN of mdk.run.tokens (volume) with agent.execute (provider attribute) -- mirrors PR #518's mdk-cost.json approach: the provider isn't on the metric, only on the span. Use the agent.execute count as the per-provider weighting basis.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
let providerWeights =
  AppDependencies
  | where Name == 'agent.execute'
  | extend cust = tostring(Properties['customer']), provider = tostring(Properties['provider'])
  | where cust in ($customer) or '$customer' == 'All'
  | summarize Runs = count() by bin(TimeGenerated, 1h), cust, provider
  | extend ProviderShare = todouble(Runs) / todouble(sum(Runs)) ;
AppMetrics
| where Name == 'mdk.run.tokens'
| extend cust = tostring(Properties['customer'])
| where cust in ($customer) or '$customer' == 'All'
| summarize Tokens = sum(ValueSum) by bin(TimeGenerated, 1h), cust
| join kind=inner providerWeights on $left.TimeGenerated == $right.TimeGenerated, $left.cust == $right.cust
| project TimeGenerated, provider, ProviderTokens = Tokens * ProviderShare
| order by TimeGenerated asc

panels.title: Tokens by provider (weighted from agent.execute.provider)

panels.description: Fleet-wide $/run = total mdk.run.cost_usd / total mdk.jobs.completed (success) per bin. Trending down is good -- model substitution, prompt-shortening, caching wins. Trending up signals model adoption shifted toward more expensive models or new agents that are more verbose.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
let cost =
  AppMetrics
  | where Name == 'mdk.run.cost_usd'
  | extend cust = tostring(Properties['customer'])
  | where cust in ($customer) or '$customer' == 'All'
  | summarize Cost = sum(ValueSum) by bin(TimeGenerated, 1h);
let runs =
  AppMetrics
  | where Name == 'mdk.jobs.completed'
  | extend cust = tostring(Properties['customer']), status = tostring(Properties['status'])
  | where cust in ($customer) or '$customer' == 'All'
  | where status == 'success'
  | summarize Runs = sum(ValueSum) by bin(TimeGenerated, 1h);
cost
| join kind=inner runs on TimeGenerated
| extend DollarsPerRun = todouble(Cost) / todouble(iff(Runs > 0, Runs, 1))
| project TimeGenerated, DollarsPerRun
| order by TimeGenerated asc

panels.title: Fleet $/run trend

panels.description: Cost outliers ranked by z-score against the customer's own 7-day median. Catches a customer who spiked relative to themselves -- not the same as 'top spender', which is the bar to the left.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
let lookback = 7d;
AppMetrics
| where Name == 'mdk.run.cost_usd'
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| extend cust = tostring(Properties['customer'])
| where cust in ($customer) or '$customer' == 'All'
| summarize HourSpend = sum(ValueSum) by bin(TimeGenerated, 1h), cust
| summarize Mean = avg(HourSpend), Std = stdev(HourSpend), Last = arg_max(TimeGenerated, HourSpend) by cust
| extend ZScore = (todouble(Last_HourSpend) - Mean) / iff(Std > 0, Std, 1.0)
| where ZScore > 2
| project Customer = cust, HourSpend = Last_HourSpend, Mean, Std, ZScore
| order by ZScore desc

panels.title: Cost outliers (z-score > 2 over 7d)

list.definition: Phase 1: Lighthouse subscription scope. Phase 2: hashed customer OTel resource attribute (ADR 039 D4).
list.label: customer
list.name: customer

title: Movate fleet -- cost (illustrative, ADR 039)
